contentBlocks.original:
दोहा
जय गणेश गिरिजा सुवन, मङ्गल करण कृपाल ।
दीनन के दुख दूर करि, कीजै नाथ निहाल ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dōhā
jaya gaṇēśa girijā suvana, maṅgala karaṇa kṛpāla ।
dīnana kē dukha dūra kari, kījai nātha nihāla ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Doha (Opening Invocation): Victory to Gaṇeśa, son of Girijā (Pārvatī), the gracious performer of all auspicious acts. O Lord, remove the sufferings of the lowly and grant us contentment.

contentBlocks.original:
जय जय श्री शनिदेव प्रभु, सुनहु विनय महाराज ।
करहु कृपा हे रवि तनय, राखहु जन की लाज ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jaya jaya śrī śanidēva prabhu, sunahu vinaya mahārāja ।
karahu kṛpā hē ravi tanaya, rākhahu jana kī lāja ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Victory, victory to Śrī Śanideva, O Lord — hear my humble prayer, O Great King. Show mercy, O son of Ravi (the Sun), and preserve the honor of your devotees.

contentBlocks.original:
चौपाई
जयति जयति शनिदेव दयाला ।
करत सदा भक्तन प्रतिपाला ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chaupāī
jayati jayati śanidēva dayālā ।
karata sadā bhaktana pratipālā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Chaupai: Glory, glory to Śanideva the compassionate, who always cherishes and protects his devotees.

contentBlocks.original:
चारि भुजा, तनु श्याम विराजै ।
माथे रतन मुकुट छवि छाजै ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chāri bhujā, tanu śyāma virājai ।
māthē ratana mukuṭa Chavi Chājai ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Four-armed, his dark form shines resplendent; on his forehead glows the brilliant jeweled crown.

contentBlocks.original:
परम विशाल मनोहर भाला ।
टेढई दृष्टि भृकुटि विकराला ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
parama viśāla manōhara bhālā ।
ṭēḍhaī dṛṣṭi bhṛkuṭi vikarālā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: His forehead is supremely broad and captivating; his sideways gaze and furrowed brow are formidable.

contentBlocks.original:
कुण्डल श्रवण चमाचम चमके ।
हिये माल मुक्तन मणि दमके ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kuṇḍala śravaṇa chamāchama chamakē ।
hiyē māla muktana maṇi damakē ॥
contentBlocks.translation: His earrings shimmer and glitter at his ears; at his chest gleams a garland of pearls and precious gems.

contentBlocks.original:
कर में गदा त्रिशूल कुठारा ।
पल बिच करैं आरिहिं संहारा ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kara mēṃ gadā triśūla kuṭhārā ।
pala bicha karaiṃ ārihiṃ saṃhārā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: In his hands he holds a mace, a trident, and an axe; in but a moment he can bring enemies to destruction.

contentBlocks.original:
पिङ्गल, कृष्णों, छाया, नन्दन ।
यम, कोणस्थ, रौद्र, दुख भञ्जन॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
piṅgala, kṛṣṇōṃ, Chāyā, nandana ।
yama, kōṇastha, raudra, dukha bhañjana॥
contentBlocks.translation: Piṅgala (the tawny-eyed), Kṛṣṇa (the dark), Chāyānandana (son of Chāyā / Saturn), Yama (the restrainer), Koṇastha (the angular), Raudra (the fierce), Dukha-bhañjana (destroyer of sorrow) —

contentBlocks.original:
सौरी, मन्द, शनि, दश नामा ।
भानु पुत्र पूजहिं सब कामा ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saurī, manda, śani, daśa nāmā ।
bhānu putra pūjahiṃ saba kāmā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Saurī (son of the Sun), Manda (the slow), Śani (Saturn) — these are ten names of the Lord. Worshipping the son of Bhānu (the Sun) fulfills all desires.

contentBlocks.original:
जा पर प्रभु प्रसन्न है जाहीम् ।
रङ्कहुं राव करैङ्क्षण माहीम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jā para prabhu prasanna hai jāhīm ।
raṅkahuṃ rāva karaiṅkṣaṇa māhīm ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Upon whomever the Lord is pleased, he can turn even a beggar into a king in a single instant.

contentBlocks.original:
पर्वतहू तृण होई निहारत ।
तृण हू को पर्वत करि डारत॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
parvatahū tṛṇa hōī nihārata ।
tṛṇa hū kō parvata kari ḍārata॥
contentBlocks.translation: Even a mountain can become a blade of grass under his gaze; and even a blade of grass he can turn into a mountain.

contentBlocks.original:
राज मिलत बन रामहिं दीन्हो ।
कैकेइहुं की मति हरि लीन्हों॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāja milata bana rāmahiṃ dīnhō ।
kaikēihuṃ kī mati hari līnhōṃ॥
contentBlocks.translation: He gave the forest to Rāma when Rāma was to receive the kingdom, and stole away the very wits of Kaikeyī.

contentBlocks.original:
बनहूं में मृग कपट दिखाई ।
मातु जानकी गी चतुराई॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
banahūṃ mēṃ mṛga kapaṭa dikhāī ।
mātu jānakī gī chaturāī॥
contentBlocks.translation: In the forest a deceptive golden deer was conjured; Mother Jānakī (Sītā) was robbed of her shrewdness and taken away.

contentBlocks.original:
लखनहिं शक्ति विकल करि डारा ।
मचिगा दल में हाहाकारा॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lakhanahiṃ śakti vikala kari ḍārā ।
machigā dala mēṃ hāhākārā॥
contentBlocks.translation: He struck Lakṣmaṇa with the Śakti dart, rendering him senseless; a great cry of lamentation and panic spread through the army.

contentBlocks.original:
रावण की गति-मति बौराई ।
रामचन्द्र सों बैर बढई॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāvaṇa kī gati-mati baurāī ।
rāmachandra sōṃ baira baḍhaī॥
contentBlocks.translation: The sense and intellect of Rāvaṇa went mad; his enmity with Śrī Rāmacandra grew without bound.

contentBlocks.original:
दियो कीट करि कञ्चन लङ्का ।
बजि बजरङ्ग बीर की डङ्का॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
diyō kīṭa kari kañchana laṅkā ।
baji bajaraṅga bīra kī ḍaṅkā॥
contentBlocks.translation: He turned golden Laṅkā into a mound fit for insects to devour; and the glory of the heroic Bajaraṅga (Hanumān) resounded across the worlds.

contentBlocks.original:
नृप विक्रम पर तुहि पगु धारा ।
चित्र मयूर निगलि गै हारा॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nṛpa vikrama para tuhi pagu dhārā ।
chitra mayūra nigali gai hārā॥
contentBlocks.translation: You set your foot upon King Vikrama (Vikramāditya); a painted peacock swallowed a priceless necklace — strange wonders unfolded.

contentBlocks.original:
हार नौलाखा लाग्यो चोरी ।
हाथ पैर डरवायो तोरी॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hāra naulākhā lāgyō chōrī ।
hātha paira ḍaravāyō tōrī॥
contentBlocks.translation: That nine-lakh necklace was charged against him as theft; in the ordeal that followed, his hands and feet were severed as punishment.

contentBlocks.original:
भारी दशा निकृष्ट दिखायो ।
तेलिहिं घर कोल्हू चलवायो॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhārī daśā nikṛṣṭa dikhāyō ।
tēlihiṃ ghara kōlhū chalavāyō॥
contentBlocks.translation: You showed him a terrible and dreadful period of affliction; the king was made to work the oil-press in an oil-presser's house.

contentBlocks.original:
विनय राग दीपक महं कीन्होम् ।
तब प्रसन्न प्रभु है सुख दीन्हों॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vinaya rāga dīpaka mahaṃ kīnhōm ।
taba prasanna prabhu hai sukha dīnhōṃ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The king sang the rāga Dīpak in humble supplication; then the Lord was pleased and granted him happiness once more.

contentBlocks.original:
हरिश्चन्द्र नृप नारि बिकानी ।
आपहुं भरे डोम घर पानी॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hariśchandra nṛpa nāri bikānī ।
āpahuṃ bharē ḍōma ghara pānī॥
contentBlocks.translation: King Hariścandra was made to sell even his queen; he himself carried water at the house of a cremation-ground keeper (Ḍoma).

contentBlocks.original:
तैसे नल परदशा सिरानी ।
भूञ्जी-मीन कूद गी पानी॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
taisē nala paradaśā sirānī ।
bhūñjī-mīna kūda gī pānī॥
contentBlocks.translation: Similarly, King Nala's period of affliction finally came to an end — like a roasted fish leaping back into the water, he was restored.

contentBlocks.original:
श्री शङ्करहि गहयो जब जाई ।
पार्वती को सती कराई॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrī śaṅkarahi gahayō jaba jāī ।
pārvatī kō satī karāī॥
contentBlocks.translation: When (Saturn) went and seized even Śrī Śaṅkara (Śiva), he caused Pārvatī to take the form of Satī and perish.

contentBlocks.original:
तनिक विलोकत ही करि रीसा ।
नभ उडि़ गयो गौरिसुत सीसा॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tanika vilōkata hī kari rīsā ।
nabha uḍi़ gayō gaurisuta sīsā॥
contentBlocks.translation: From just a brief sideways glance — and in his anger — the head of Gaurī's son (Gaṇeśa) flew up into the sky.

contentBlocks.original:
पाण्डव पर भै दशा तुम्हारी ।
बची द्रौपदी होति उघारी॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pāṇḍava para bhai daśā tumhārī ।
bachī draupadī hōti ughārī॥
contentBlocks.translation: Your afflicting period fell upon the Pāṇḍavas; Draupadī was saved just as she was being dragged and disrobed.

contentBlocks.original:
कौरव के भी गति मति मारयो ।
युद्घ महाभारत करि डारयो॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kaurava kē bhī gati mati mārayō ।
yudgha mahābhārata kari ḍārayō॥
contentBlocks.translation: You destroyed the sense and intellect of the Kauravas as well, and caused the great war of the Mahābhārata to be fought.

contentBlocks.original:
रवि कहं मुख महं धरि तत्काला ।
लेकर कूदि परयो पाताला ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ravi kahaṃ mukha mahaṃ dhari tatkālā ।
lēkara kūdi parayō pātālā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Śani instantly took the Sun (Ravi) into his mouth and, holding him there, leaped down into the netherworld (Pātāla).

contentBlocks.original:
शेष देव-लखि विनती लाई ।
रवि को मुख ते दियो छुडई ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śēṣa dēva-lakhi vinatī lāī ।
ravi kō mukha tē diyō Chuḍaī ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Seeing this, the remaining gods came and prayed; and (Śani) released the Sun from his mouth.

contentBlocks.original:
वाहन प्रभु के सात सुजाना ।
जग दिग्ज गर्दभ मृग स्वाना ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vāhana prabhu kē sāta sujānā ।
jaga digja gardabha mṛga svānā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The Lord has seven well-known mounts: the great elephant, the vulture, the donkey, the deer, and the dog —

contentBlocks.original:
जम्बुक सिंह आदि नखधारी ।
सो फल जज्योतिष कहत पुकारी ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jambuka siṃha ādi nakhadhārī ।
sō phala jajyōtiṣa kahata pukārī ॥
contentBlocks.translation: — the jackal, the lion, and other clawed creatures. These mounts each bestow certain results, as astrologers proclaim aloud.

contentBlocks.original:
गज वाहन लक्ष्मी गृह आवैम् ।
हय ते सुख सम्पत्ति उपजावैम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gaja vāhana lakṣmī gṛha āvaim ।
haya tē sukha sampatti upajāvaim ॥
contentBlocks.translation: When mounted on the elephant, Lakṣmī comes to the home; from the horse-mount, happiness and wealth arise.

contentBlocks.original:
गर्दभ हानि करै बहु काजा ।
गर्दभ सिद्घ कर राज समाजा ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gardabha hāni karai bahu kājā ।
gardabha sidgha kara rāja samājā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The donkey-mount causes harm in many endeavors; yet the donkey-mount can also establish one among the kingly assembly.

contentBlocks.original:
जम्बुक बुद्घि नष्ट कर डारै ।
मृग दे कष्ट प्रण संहारै ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jambuka budghi naṣṭa kara ḍārai ।
mṛga dē kaṣṭa praṇa saṃhārai ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The jackal-mount destroys one's intellect entirely; the deer-mount brings suffering and may even take one's life.

contentBlocks.original:
जब आवहिं प्रभु स्वान सवारी ।
चोरी आदि होय डर भारी ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jaba āvahiṃ prabhu svāna savārī ।
chōrī ādi hōya ḍara bhārī ॥
contentBlocks.translation: When the Lord arrives riding on the dog, there is great fear of theft and similar misfortunes.

contentBlocks.original:
तैसहि चारि चरण यह नामा ।
स्वर्ण लौह चाञ्जी अरु तामा ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
taisahi chāri charaṇa yaha nāmā ।
svarṇa lauha chāñjī aru tāmā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Similarly there are four "feet" (phases) of Śani's cycle by these names: Gold (Svarṇa), Iron (Lauha), Silver (Cāñdī), and Copper (Tāmā).

contentBlocks.original:
लौह चरण पर जब प्रभु आवैम् ।
धन जन सम्पत्ति नष्ट करावै ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lauha charaṇa para jaba prabhu āvaim ।
dhana jana sampatti naṣṭa karāvai ॥
contentBlocks.translation: When the Lord arrives in his iron-foot phase, he causes wealth, people, and all prosperity to be destroyed.

contentBlocks.original:
समता ताम्र रजत शुभकारी ।
स्वर्ण सर्व सुख मङ्गल कारी ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samatā tāmra rajata śubhakārī ।
svarṇa sarva sukha maṅgala kārī ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The copper and silver feet bring balance and auspiciousness; the golden foot brings all happiness and every blessing.

contentBlocks.original:
जो यह शनि चरित्र नित गावै ।
कबहुं न दशा निकृष्ट सतावै ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jō yaha śani charitra nita gāvai ।
kabahuṃ na daśā nikṛṣṭa satāvai ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever sings this Śani-carita (account of Śani) daily shall never be tormented by a terrible period of affliction.

contentBlocks.original:
अदभुत नाथ दिखावैं लीला ।
करैं शत्रु के नशि बलि ढीला ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
adabhuta nātha dikhāvaiṃ līlā ।
karaiṃ śatru kē naśi bali ḍhīlā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The wondrous Lord displays his divine play; he renders the enemy's power weak and destroys it utterly.

contentBlocks.original:
जो पण्डित सुयोग्य बुलवाई ।
विधिवत शनि ग्रह शान्ति कराई ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jō paṇḍita suyōgya bulavāī ।
vidhivata śani graha śānti karāī ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever summons a learned and qualified Paṇḍita and performs a proper Śani-graha śānti (propitiatory rite) —

contentBlocks.original:
पीपल जल शनि दिवस चढावत ।
दीप दान दै बहु सुख पावत ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pīpala jala śani divasa chaḍhāvata ।
dīpa dāna dai bahu sukha pāvata ॥
contentBlocks.translation: — who offers water to the Pīpala tree on Saturday and gives the gift of a lamp — attains abundant happiness.

contentBlocks.original:
कहत रामसुन्दर प्रभु दासा ।
शनि सुमिरत सुख होत प्रकाशा ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kahata rāmasundara prabhu dāsā ।
śani sumirata sukha hōta prakāśā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Says Rāmasundara, the servant of the Lord: Remembering Śani, happiness and light arise within.

contentBlocks.original:
दोहा
पाठ शनिश्चर देव को, की हों विमल तैयार ।
करत पाठ चालीस दिन, हो भवसागर पार ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dōhā
pāṭha śaniśchara dēva kō, kī hōṃ vimala taiyāra ।
karata pāṭha chālīsa dina, hō bhavasāgara pāra ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Doha (Closing): Taking up the recitation of Śaniścara Deva, having first prepared oneself in purity — reciting it for forty days, one crosses the ocean of worldly existence.
